rulings.Case Title: CROWDSTRIKE, INC. VS. DFND SECURITY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 25, 2026, line 1. PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. Denied as moot. By companion order, the court has denied the petition to confirm arbitration and has vacated the award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CROWDSTRIKE, INC. VS. DFND SECURITY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 25, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER CROWDSTRIKE, INC.'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner CrowdStrike, Inc's petition to confirm arbitration award is denied pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 1286.2. The arbitration award is therefore vacated. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Amend Judgment To Add T24 Llc, Legal Recovery Llc, And Legal Recover Llc As Judgment Debtors [Ccp Section 473 Subd. (D)] (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 25, 2026, line 3. THIRD PARTY T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, VI TRAN'S Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Amend Judgment To Add T24 LLC, Legal Recovery LLC, And Legal Recover LLC As Judgment Debtors [CCP Section 473 Subd. (D)]. Third parties T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, and VI TRAN, move this court to set aside its order of May 28, 2025 adding T24 LLC, Legal Recovery LLC, and Legal Recover LLC as judgment debtors in this action. This order was appealed on July 28, 2025. This court therefore lacks jurisdiction over the order; "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 916, subd. (a).) Moving parties rely on the rule that if a judgment "is void upon its face, it may be set aside at any time," regardless of whether an appeal is pending. (Fallon v. Superior Court of City and County of San Francisco (1939) 33 Cal.App.2d 48, 51.) But the order they seek to set aside is not void on its face; moving parties seek to set aside the order based on extrinsic evidence, to wit, information provided in the Leeds declaration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

DEFENDANTS RICK BELGARDE, KAREN KELLER, CFO RICK INC.'S Motion For Interpleader Direction, Discharge Of Stakeholder And Reasonable Attorneys Fees. In light of the bankruptcy stay, the court continues this fully-briefed matter for hearing to August 25, 2026. rulings.Case Title: SAID ANWARI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To Deeming Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant Sayed Hazratullahs Request Be Deemed Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 25, 2026, line 7. PLAINTIFF SAID ANWARI'S Motion For Order To Deeming Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant Sayed Hazratullahs Request Be Deemed Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Said Anwari's motion for a deemed-admitted order as to plaintiff's first set of requests for admissions to defendant Sayed Hazratullah is granted. A deemed-admitted when a party fails to timely respond is mandatory. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subds. (b) and (c).) That Anwari is not in contact with counsel is not a basis to deny this mandatory order. Parties have duties to submit to authorized methods of discovery. In addition, sanctions are mandatory. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders Hazratullah to pay $2,240 to Anwari within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. KING ARTHUR BAKING COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 25, 2026, line 8. PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, SOLELY IN ITS CAPACITY AS TRUSTEE'S Motion For Order Appointing Referee. The unopposed motion for appointment of Hon. David A. Garcia (ret.) as referee is granted. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 638.) Judge Garcia's contact information is JAMS, 2 Embarcadero Center, Ste 1500, San Francisco, CA 94111, (415) 774-2607. His state bar number is 48963. The reference covers all issues in dispute. Pursuant ot Section 12 of the Guaranty of Recourse Obligations, defendant Robert A.
